subtitle: A shallow turquoise lagoon among desert cacti near La Paz — repeatedly voted Mexico's most beautiful beach, protected against all development, capped by daily reservation, and anchored by El Hongo, the balanced 'mushroom' rock a tourist once toppled.
spike_statement: A shallow turquoise desert lagoon near La Paz, protected so tightly it bans hotels and caps daily visitors, anchored by El Hongo, a balanced rock once broken and rebuilt.

levers.photo_tone: impossibly shallow turquoise water over pale sandbanks, cactus-studded desert hills meeting the Sea of Cortez, the balanced mushroom silhouette of El Hongo against a bright Baja sky
